title: Fix reverse-proxy palette item creating invisible node
description: The palette-items.ts has a 'reverse-proxy' type in the Load Balancing category, but systemDesignNodeTypes in index.ts has no 'reverse-proxy' key. Dragging this item creates a node React Flow can't render — silent failure. Either add ReverseProxyNode component + register in nodeTypes, or change palette type to 'cdn' which already has a component.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging 'Reverse Proxy' from palette creates a visible, interactive node on canvas
Node renders correctly at all 3 LOD tiers (dot, simplified, full)

title: Verify and fix auth-service palette type mapping
description: palette-items.ts has 'auth-service' type but it's unclear if it maps to a nodeType. The security category also has 'auth-service-v2'. Verify the mapping and either add the missing nodeType or update the palette type.
acceptanceCriteria: auth-service palette item creates a renderable node
No duplicate or confusing naming between auth-service and auth-service-v2

title: Stop simulation and clean up on module switch
description: When user switches from system-design to another module via activity bar, simulation continues running in background — particles render, metrics update, rAF loops spin. ui-store.ts:setActiveModule() has no cleanup hook. Add: on module switch away from system-design, call simulation-store stop(), clear SimMetricsBus, cancel ParticleLayer rAF.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching to another module while simulation is running stops the simulation
No rAF loops or subscriptions continue after module switch
Returning to system-design shows idle state, not stale running state

title: Add cdn and client items to component palette
description: systemDesignNodeTypes has 'cdn' and 'client' keys with working components (CDNNode, ClientNode). These are used in 14 and 15 templates respectively. But palette-items.ts has no entry for either — users can't create these nodes from the sidebar. Add palette entries for both.
acceptanceCriteria: CDN component appears in palette under Networking category
Client component appears in palette under Client category
Both can be dragged to canvas and render correctly

title: Align web-client palette type with client nodeType
description: palette-items.ts has 'web-client' type but nodeTypes uses 'client'. When user drags 'Web Client', the created node has type:'web-client' which React Flow looks up as nodeTypes['web-client'] — likely undefined. Verify and fix the mapping chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging 'Web Client' from palette creates a working node
Node type string is consistent between palette and nodeTypes registry

title: Extract shared getUtilizationColor() utility
description: Utilization color thresholds are hardcoded in 5+ files with INCONSISTENT values: TimeTravelScrubber uses 0.85/0.6, NodeMetricsOverlay uses 0.9/0.7, CostMonitor uses 0.9/0.7, HeatmapOverlay uses 0.8/0.5, SimMetricsBadge uses 0.85/0.6. Extract to a single shared function in src/lib/utils or src/lib/constants/thresholds.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Single getUtilizationColor(value) function used by all 5+ consumers
Consistent thresholds across all components
Returns CSS variable references, not hardcoded hex

title: Implement transaction-based undo for canvas drag operations
description: canvas-store.ts pushSnapshot() on every onNodesChange/onEdgesChange (lines 118-124), including each pixel of a drag. This creates dozens of undo entries per drag. Implement drag-transaction: call pushSnapshot once at dragStart, skip during drag, restore if needed. STATE_ARCHITECTURE.ts already documents this approach at lines 1438-1590.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a node creates exactly 1 undo entry
Undo after drag returns node to pre-drag position
Undo after editing a config field returns the previous value

title: Build LearnSteps panel to surface template educational content
description: All 55 templates have learnSteps arrays with educational content (highlighted nodes, explanations, step-through guides), but NO UI component renders them. Create a LearnSteps panel that shows step-by-step explanations, highlights relevant nodes/edges on the canvas, and guides the user through understanding the architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: After loading a template, a 'Learn' button or panel is visible
Clicking through steps highlights relevant nodes and shows explanations
User can navigate forward/backward through steps
Current step is visually indicated (progress bar or dots)

title: Add difficulty filter and sort to template gallery
description: Templates have difficulty ratings (1-5) in their JSON but the template gallery UI shows no difficulty badges, filtering, or sorting. Add: difficulty stars/badge on each template card, filter buttons or dropdown for difficulty level, sort options (difficulty, name, category).
acceptanceCriteria: Each template card shows its difficulty rating
User can filter templates by difficulty level
Default sort shows easier templates first for beginners

title: Add first-time simulation onboarding flow
description: Simulation features (play, chaos injection, what-if, time travel, heatmap) are powerful but undiscoverable. Add a contextual onboarding flow that triggers after a user's first template load: highlight the Play button, explain the simulation dashboard, introduce chaos injection with a guided example.
acceptanceCriteria: First-time users see a 3-5 step onboarding after loading their first template
Onboarding highlights Play button, explains dashboard, shows chaos drag
Onboarding can be dismissed and does not reappear
Onboarding state persisted in localStorage

title: Add cycle detection to LatencyBudget critical path algorithm
description: latency-budget.ts line 187: Kahn's algorithm falls back to first node as source if all nodes have in-degree > 0 (cycle). This produces an invalid partial path without error. Add explicit cycle detection: if sources array is empty and unvisited nodes remain, return an error with the cycle nodes listed.
acceptanceCriteria: Cyclic graphs return an error result with cycle information
Error is surfaced in the UI (e.g., toast or warning in metrics panel)
Non-cyclic graphs continue to work as before

title: Add metric tooltips explaining what throughput/latency/errorRate mean
description: Mayer's Pre-training principle: simulation shows throughput, latency, error rate numbers without explaining what they mean or what good/bad values look like. Add hover tooltips to all metric displays explaining: what the metric measures, what a healthy range is, what indicates a problem.
acceptanceCriteria: Every metric label in SimulationDashboard has an info tooltip
Tooltips explain the metric in beginner-friendly language
Tooltips include threshold context (e.g., 'Below 200ms is good for most APIs')

title: Fix HeatmapOverlay broad Zustand selector causing excess re-renders
description: HeatmapOverlay.tsx subscribes via useCanvasStore(s => s.nodes) which triggers re-render on ANY node change. During simulation, nodes update every tick causing 60+ re-renders/sec. Use a targeted selector: either subscribe to simulation-store metrics or use useShallow to only re-render when node positions change.
acceptanceCriteria: HeatmapOverlay re-renders only when metric values change, not on every tick
Visual behavior unchanged — heatmap still updates during simulation
Verified via React DevTools Profiler

title: Consolidate simulation metrics into unified view
description: Split-attention effect (Sweller CLT): during simulation, metrics are shown in 4 separate locations — SimulationDashboard (top-right), NodeMetricsBadges (on nodes), CostMonitor (bottom-left), BottomPanel metrics tab. Users must scan 4 areas simultaneously. Redesign: keep node-level badges but consolidate global metrics into a single expandable dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Global simulation metrics are visible in one location
Node-level badges remain for per-component detail
User can access all metrics without looking at 4+ locations

title: Move SimMetricsBadge CSS rules to global stylesheet
description: SimMetricsBadge.tsx lines 163-168 injects a <style> tag with ::after rules per component instance. With 50 nodes, this creates 50 identical <style> tags. Move these 4 CSS rules to globals.css.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS rules for .sim-badge-cpu::after etc. are in globals.css
No <style> tag injected per component instance
Metrics badges still display correctly

title: Add edge type color legend to canvas
description: ParticleLayer uses different colors for different edge types (http=blue, grpc=purple, websocket=green-dashed, etc.) but there's no legend. Users can't tell what the colors mean. Add a small collapsible legend showing edge type colors + labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Legend is visible during simulation (small, bottom-right or expandable)
Shows all 9 edge type colors with their names
Can be collapsed to save space

title: Add tap-to-inject alternative for ChaosQuickBar on mobile
description: ChaosQuickBar only supports drag-and-drop to inject chaos events. On mobile, there's no tap alternative. Add: tap a chaos event to select it, then tap a node to inject. Or tap event + auto-inject on a random eligible node.
acceptanceCriteria: Chaos events can be injected via tap on touch devices
Visual feedback shows which event is selected before injection
Drag-and-drop still works on desktop

title: Add spatial indexing for AlignmentGuides to prevent jank at scale
description: AlignmentGuides.tsx findAlignmentGuides() performs O(n*m) comparisons for each dragged node against all other nodes every frame. At 100+ nodes, this causes drag jank. Implement a simple grid-based spatial index or only compare against the 10 nearest nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a node in a 200-node canvas is smooth (60fps)
Alignment guides still appear correctly for nearby nodes
No visual regression in guide accuracy

title: Move ensureKeyframes() from useMemo to useEffect in BaseNode
description: BaseNode.tsx line 262: ensureKeyframes() is a DOM side effect called inside useMemo with [] deps. This violates React rules — useMemo should be side-effect-free. Move to useEffect or useInsertionEffect.
acceptanceCriteria: Keyframes are injected on first mount, not during render
No duplicate keyframe injection
Animations still work correctly

title: Fix mutable render-time counters in CanvasToolbar and ComponentPalette
description: CanvasToolbar uses _tabIdx and ComponentPalette uses flatIndex as mutable variables during render. With React 18+ concurrent features, render can be interrupted and restarted, causing stale or inconsistent counter values. Use useRef or pre-compute the indexed array before render.
acceptanceCriteria: No mutable variables mutated during render phase
Keyboard navigation still works correctly in both components
Compatible with React concurrent mode

title: Show error toast on DiffPanel bad JSON import instead of silent fail
description: DiffPanel.tsx FileReader.onerror and JSON.parse failures are silently swallowed. User imports a bad file, nothing happens. Show a toast: 'Invalid JSON file. Please check the file format.'
acceptanceCriteria: Importing an invalid JSON file shows an error toast
Importing a valid JSON file still works as before
Error message is user-friendly, not technical

title: Add export feedback — toast notification on success/failure
description: CanvasToolbar export buttons (PNG, SVG, PDF, JSON, etc.) have empty catch blocks. No feedback on success or failure. Add toast notifications: 'Exported as PNG' on success, 'Export failed: [reason]' on error.
acceptanceCriteria: Every export action shows a success toast with format name
Export failures show an error toast with actionable message
No empty catch blocks remain

title: Show 'Add components first' when running simulation on empty canvas
description: Running simulation with 0 nodes on canvas shows SimulationDashboard with all-zero metrics — silent no-op. Guard play(): if nodes.length === 0, show toast 'Add components to your design first' and don't start simulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Play button on empty canvas shows a helpful message instead of starting
Message is user-friendly and suggests what to do
Simulation starts normally when nodes exist

title: Add 'Try next' template suggestions after viewing one
description: After loading a template, no suggestion for what to explore next. Add related template recommendations based on: similar difficulty, same category, or logical next step (e.g., after URL Shortener → suggest Search Engine or Chat System).
acceptanceCriteria: After loading a template, a small 'Try next' section shows 2-3 related templates
Recommendations are relevant (same category or progressive difficulty)
Clicking a recommendation loads that template

title: Add completion badges to template gallery cards
description: No progress tracking for templates. Users can't see which templates they've explored, simulated, or completed challenges on. Add completion state: 'viewed', 'simulated', 'completed' badges on gallery cards using localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Template cards show a subtle completion indicator (checkmark, progress ring, or badge)
State persists across sessions via localStorage
At least 3 states: unseen, viewed, simulated

title: Add simulation configs to remaining 40 templates
description: Only 15 of 55 templates have simulation sections. The remaining 40 lack chaos scenarios, bottleneck progression, cost breakdown, and monitoring config. Add simulation configs to at least the top 20 most common templates (authentication, chat, social feed, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: At least 35 of 55 templates have simulation configs
Each config includes: chaos scenarios, cost breakdown, performance targets
Simulations run successfully on each updated template

title: Create templates using fintech, AI/LLM, and data-engineering node types
description: Templates only use 23 of 72 available node types. Newer categories (fintech: payment-gateway/ledger/fraud-detection/hsm; AI: llm-gateway/tool-registry/memory-fabric; data-eng: etl-pipeline/cdc-service/schema-registry) are never used. Create 5+ new templates showcasing these.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 fintech templates (e.g., payment processing, fraud detection)
At least 2 AI/LLM templates (e.g., RAG pipeline, agent orchestration)
At least 1 data engineering template (e.g., real-time ETL pipeline)
Templates include learnSteps and simulation configs

title: Extract generateNodeId() to shared utility
description: generateNodeId() is duplicated in DesignCanvas.tsx:62 and CanvasContextMenu.tsx with identical Date.now()+Math.random() pattern. Extract to src/lib/utils.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Single generateNodeId() in shared utility
Both DesignCanvas and CanvasContextMenu import from the same source
No duplicate definitions

title: Replace CanvasContextMenu hardcoded component list with palette-items import
description: CanvasContextMenu has a hardcoded list of 8 quick-add component types that duplicates data from palette-items.ts. Import the top items from palette-items instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Quick-add submenu items derived from palette-items.ts
No hardcoded component list in CanvasContextMenu
Quick-add still shows the most common components

title: Replace global clipboard with Zustand store in NodeContextMenu
description: NodeContextMenu uses a module-level mutable variable for clipboard (getNodeClipboard). Replace with a clipboard slice in canvas-store or ui-store for proper state management.
acceptanceCriteria: Clipboard state is managed by Zustand store, not global variable
Copy/cut/paste still work correctly
Clipboard state is accessible from both CanvasContextMenu and NodeContextMenu

title: Move STATE_ARCHITECTURE.ts from src/stores/ to docs/adr/
description: STATE_ARCHITECTURE.ts is a 1806-line design document living in the runtime src/stores/ directory. It's not imported by any runtime code but adds to bundle analysis noise. Move to docs/adr/ where other architecture decisions live.
acceptanceCriteria: File moved to docs/adr/STATE_ARCHITECTURE.md (or .ts kept as-is)
No runtime imports break
Build size unchanged or smaller

title: Migrate hardcoded colors in simulation overlays to CSS custom properties
description: 12 instances of hardcoded hex/HSL colors across: BaseNode.tsx (state glows, keyframes), AlignmentGuides.tsx (#E879F9), ParticleLayer.tsx (EDGE_TYPE_COLORS), HeatmapOverlay.tsx (three-stop colors), TimeTravelScrubber.tsx, NodeCreationPulse.tsx. Replace with CSS custom properties for theming.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex/HSL color values in simulation overlay components
All colors reference CSS custom properties
Dark and light themes both look correct

title: Increase minimum font sizes to 11px across module
description: 3 components use font sizes below the 11px minimum: SimMetricsBadge (9px at line 142), BaseNode simplified label (10px at line 307), DataFlowEdge labels (10px at lines 148, 166, 183). Increase all to at least 11px.
acceptanceCriteria: No text smaller than 11px in any component
Layout still works without overflow at the larger sizes
Verified visually at all zoom levels

title: Lazy-load chaos-engine.ts on simulation start
description: chaos-engine.ts is 1521 lines loaded at module init. It's only needed when simulation starts. Convert to dynamic import: const { ChaosEngine } = await import('@/lib/simulation/chaos-engine') inside play().
acceptanceCriteria: chaos-engine.ts is not in the initial bundle for system-design module
First simulation start lazy-loads the engine
Subsequent simulation starts reuse the cached import

title: Add ARIA attributes to TimeTravelScrubber
description: TimeTravelScrubber acts as a slider but has no aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, aria-label. Screen readers can't convey the current simulation position. Add proper ARIA attributes to the scrub track.
acceptanceCriteria: Scrub track has role='slider', aria-valuemin=0, aria-valuemax=totalTicks, aria-valuenow=currentTick
aria-label describes purpose: 'Simulation timeline scrubber'
Screen reader announces tick position changes

title: Add color-independent indicators for HeatmapOverlay
description: HeatmapOverlay uses green/yellow/red color gradient with no text or shape backup. Users with color vision deficiency can't distinguish utilization levels. Add numeric labels or pattern fills alongside color.
acceptanceCriteria: Utilization levels distinguishable without color (numbers or patterns visible)
Legend includes both color and text values
Passes WCAG 2.1 Level AA for color independence

title: Add ChaosEngine unit tests for inject/expire/clear lifecycle
description: ChaosEngine has no unit tests. Add tests covering: injectEvent with valid/invalid type IDs, event expiry via expireEvents(), clearAll(), getEventsForNode(), catalog lookup, and instance ID generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: inject, expire, clear, catalog lookup, invalid inputs
Edge cases: inject on non-existent node type, expire with no events, inject with null duration
All tests pass

title: Add LatencyBudget unit tests with known graph topology
description: LatencyBudget critical path algorithm has no tests. Add tests with: known linear graph (A→B→C), DAG with multiple paths, graph with cycle (should error per SDS-131), empty graph, single node.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: linear path, branching path, longest path selection, cycle detection, empty input
Latency values verified mathematically
All tests pass

title: Add SimMetricsBus unit tests for write/read/subscribe cycle
description: SimMetricsBus is a critical zero-alloc hot-path component with no tests. Add tests: write and read a metric, verify buffer values, test subscriber batching via rAF mock, test MAX_NODES overflow handling, test reset().
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: write/read round-trip, multiple nodes, subscriber notification, reset, overflow
Verifies Float64Array buffer values are correct
All tests pass

title: Add integration test for canvas drag-and-drop node creation
description: No test verifies the full palette→canvas drag-drop flow. Add: simulate drop event with application/architex-node MIME data, verify node appears in canvas-store, verify React Flow renders the node. Also test with malformed data (should not crash).
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies node appears after valid drop
Test verifies graceful handling of malformed drop data
Test verifies node type matches expected component

title: Add input size guard to prevent memory exhaustion via unlimited nodes
description: No cap on canvas node count. Users (or templates) can create unlimited nodes → memory exhaustion. Add a MAX_NODES constant (e.g., 500) with warning toast when approaching limit and hard block at limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Warning toast at 80% of MAX_NODES
Hard block at MAX_NODES with helpful message
Existing designs with fewer nodes unaffected

title: Split CanvasToolbar into focused subcomponents
description: CanvasToolbar.tsx is 797 lines with simulation controls, export menu, view toggles, and tool selection all in one component. Split into: ToolGroup (select/pan/connect), SimulationControls (play/pause/stop/speed), ExportMenu, ViewControls (minimap/timeline/heatmap).
acceptanceCriteria: CanvasToolbar.tsx reduced to <200 lines composing subcomponents
Each subcomponent is independently testable
Visual behavior unchanged

title: Add keyboard shortcuts to simulation controls (play/pause without toolbar)
description: Space bar plays/pauses simulation (via use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts), but chaos injection, speed control, and trace activation have no shortcuts. ChaosQuickBar has no keyboard interaction at all. Add keyboard shortcuts for common simulation actions.
acceptanceCriteria: ChaosQuickBar items navigable via keyboard (arrow keys + Enter to inject)
Speed control accessible via keyboard shortcut (e.g., ] to speed up, [ to slow down)
All shortcuts documented in keyboard shortcuts dialog

title: Add structured learning path UI to template gallery
description: Templates have difficulty ratings but no recommended path. Add a 'Learning Path' view to the gallery showing templates organized by difficulty with prerequisites and 'Start Here' designation. Curriculum: Beginner (URL Shortener, DNS, Rate Limiter) → Intermediate (Chat, Social Feed, Search Engine) → Advanced (Netflix CDN, Payment System) → Expert (Uber Dispatch, Event Sourcing).
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery has a 'Learning Path' toggle/tab view
Templates grouped by difficulty with clear level labels
A 'Start Here' indicator for first-time users
Prerequisites visible (e.g., 'Learn Load Balancer first')

title: Add 'predict before reveal' mode to simulation for active learning
description: Mayer's segmenting + germane load: Instead of passive animation watching, add a mode where the simulation pauses at key moments and asks 'What happens next?' The user predicts (e.g., 'cache miss → database query') and gets feedback. Based on research showing active prediction improves learning 2-3x over passive observation.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation has a 'Learn Mode' toggle that pauses at interesting moments
At each pause, user sees a prediction prompt with 2-3 options
Correct predictions show green feedback; incorrect show explanation
User can skip prediction mode for free-run simulation

title: Add one-click GIF/video export of simulation animation
description: Research shows the #1 reason algorithm/system design visualizers go viral is shareable animations. Add a 'Record' button that captures the simulation as a GIF or MP4. Use MediaRecorder API on canvas + DOM overlay compositing.
acceptanceCriteria: Record button visible during simulation
Captures canvas particles + node states as animation
Exports as GIF or MP4 (user choice)
Recording stops automatically when simulation ends

title: Add AI architecture critique — flag SPOFs and missing components
description: Research finding: no existing tool validates/critiques designs. Add an 'Audit' button that analyzes the current canvas and flags: single points of failure, missing load balancers, databases without caching, no monitoring/observability, missing auth. Rules-based engine (no LLM required).
acceptanceCriteria: Audit button analyzes current architecture
Flags at least 5 common anti-patterns (SPOF, no cache, no LB, no monitoring, no auth)
Results shown as actionable suggestions with affected nodes highlighted
Can be dismissed or acted on individually

title: Add sonification — hear the difference between healthy and overloaded systems
description: Never-been-done: Map simulation metrics to audio using Web Audio API. Healthy system = calm ambient tone. Overloaded = rising pitch/distortion. Cache hits = soft pops. Errors = dissonant stings. Toggle on/off. Makes the FEELING of O(n) vs O(n^2) tangible through sound.
acceptanceCriteria: Sound toggle in simulation controls (default off)
Throughput mapped to tempo (more RPS = faster rhythm)
Errors produce distinctive alert sounds
Works with prefers-reduced-motion (visual-only fallback)

title: Add 'Break My System' challenge mode — find the worst-case input
description: Inversion technique: instead of 'watch how it works', challenge user to BREAK it. 'Find the traffic pattern that causes this system to fail'. User configures traffic/chaos events, system scores how quickly they found the failure. Gamifies understanding of failure modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Challenge mode accessible from template view
User configures traffic/chaos to try to break the system
System detects failure conditions (error rate > 10%, latency > SLA)
Score shown: time to failure, number of events used, creativity bonus

title: Add side-by-side architecture comparison mode
description: Cross-domain transfer from sports drills: compare two approaches. Load two templates side-by-side, run identical traffic, see which performs better. E.g., 'URL Shortener with cache vs without cache' or 'Monolith vs Microservices under same load'.
acceptanceCriteria: Split-screen view showing two architectures
Both run identical simulation traffic simultaneously
Metrics comparison table auto-generated (throughput, latency, cost, error rate)
Winner/loser indicators per metric

title: Add keyboard shortcut guard for contentEditable elements
description: use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts attaches global keyboard handlers without checking if focus is in a contentEditable element or text input. Typing in the Properties panel config fields or node rename input could accidentally trigger shortcuts (e.g., pressing 'Space' plays simulation instead of typing a space).
acceptanceCriteria: Keyboard shortcuts do not fire when focus is in an input, textarea, or contentEditable element
Shortcuts still work when focus is on canvas or toolbar
No regression in existing shortcut behavior
